description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, constructed from vertically aligned dark wooden staves held together by four black metal hoops—two near the top and bottom, and two at the midsection. The wood appears aged and weathered, with visible grain and some discoloration, suggesting long-term use or outdoor exposure. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood as the sides, with the top slightly recessed within the rim. One side of the barrel features a faded black marking, including a bat-like symbol and partial text, giving it a rustic, possibly vintage or branded appearance. The metal hoops are fastened with visible rivets, providing structural reinforcement and a traditional look. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleyways, storage yards, or near pubs and restaurants, often used for storage or decorative purposes.
description: A weathered wooden barrel reinforced with black metal hoops, featuring faded markings and a rustic, vintage appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the faded black marking with a bat symbol and partial text, as well as the wood grain and all four metal hoops.
description_view_1: This side view displays the barrel's wood texture and metal hoops, but no visible markings or text.
description_view_2: Another side view with a clear look at the wood staves and metal hoops, lacking any visible markings.
description_view_3: This view highlights the barrel's curvature and metal hoops, with no visible markings or text.
attribute_labeling_or_markings: faded black marking with bat symbol and partial text
